title: Neomammillaria verhaertiana
taxonomy: I was able to find the classification for Neomammillaria verhaertiana. Neomammillaria verhaertiana belongs to the kingdom Plantae, further classified under the family Cactaceae, and the genus Mammillaria. It is a type of cactus native to Mexico. This classification has been supported by various botanical sources. Neomammillaria verhaertiana is now considered to be a synonym of Mammillaria verhaertiana.

intro: Neomammillaria verhaertiana, also known as the 'Night-Blooming Pincushion Cactus', is a species of flowering cactus native to Mexico. This plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria verhaertiana include flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, alkaloids like veratrum and solanine, and saponins like oleanolic acid and ursolic acid. The plant is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and wounds.
